name: Chronic Kidney Disease - Reduced eGFR Lab Query
description: Tests ability to find chronic kidney disease patients via reduced eGFR. The Columbia CKD V4.2 algorithm uses eGFR (CKD-EPI formula) as the primary staging variable: <60 mL/min/1.73 m² indicates CKD Stage 3 or worse. This test case uses a less-than threshold (lt60) and exercises the most quantitative phenotype in our suite, where staging is entirely lab-driven.
prompt: Find laboratory observations for estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) where the value is below 60 mL/min/1.73 m², indicating chronic kidney disease stage 3 or worse.

prompts.broad: Find Observation resources for estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) measurements where the value is less than 60 mL/min/1.73 m². An eGFR below 60 sustained for at least 3 months meets KDIGO criteria for chronic kidney disease stage 3 or higher.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources for reduced eGFR. Use LOINC 62238-1 (Glomerular filtration rate by Creatinine-based formula CKD-EPI) — this is the algorithm-specified equation. Apply a value-quantity filter with the lt prefix at threshold 60. The query should look like: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|62238-1&value-quantity=lt60. Note that LOINC 33914-3 (eGFR by MDRD) and 48642-3/48643-1 (race-based variants) may also appear in some data; the CKD-EPI code is preferred per current KDIGO 2012 guidelines.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with reduced eGFR (Paths A and C). Tests the value-quantity less-than filter pattern with a quantitative threshold tied directly to disease staging. The eGFR < 60 cutoff is the binary threshold separating CKD Stage 1-2 from Stage 3-5.

required_codes.display: Glomerular filtration rate [Volume Rate/Area] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Creatinine-based formula (CKD-EPI)/1.73 sq M
